meta.keywords: Yogyakarta 4 days tour Malaysia, Borobudur tour package, Solo Surakarta tour, Sangiran UNESCO, Mount Merapi jeep, best seller Yogyakarta, Indonesia tour from KL

hero.title: 4 Days Yogyakarta
hero.intro: Java's best-selling Yogyakarta itinerary — combining the world's greatest Buddhist temple at Borobudur, a heart-pounding Merapi Jeep Lava Tour, and an immersive heritage journey through the royal city of Solo (Surakarta) including a UNESCO prehistoric site. All on a fully private tour from just RM1,288 per person.
hero.heroImageAlt: Borobudur temple at dawn, Yogyakarta, Indonesia

quickFacts.saving: Save RM400 per couple with group discounts

overview.heading: 4 Days Covering Yogyakarta's Greatest Hits — Plus a Solo Heritage Day
body: This is our most popular Yogyakarta package and it's easy to understand why. Four days and three nights give you enough time to properly absorb the majesty of Borobudur, feel the volcanic energy of Mount Merapi up close, relax along the southern coast at sunset, and discover the ancient royal city of Solo (Surakarta) — one of Java's most underrated cultural gems. The inclusion of the UNESCO-listed Sangiran Early Man Site makes this package especially popular with families and history enthusiasts.

All tours are conducted on a 100% private basis — your own English-speaking driver-guide and comfortable private vehicle. Groups of 5 or more enjoy significant discounts (RM1,188/person), and groups of 8 or more qualify for our best rate of RM1,088/person. The package is valid until March 2027, with hotel options ranging from well-located 3-star Malioboro area hotels to stylish 4-star boutique properties.

items.title: Borobudur — The World's Largest Buddhist Temple
items.body: A UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1991, Borobudur's nine-tiered stone pyramid rises from the Kedu Plain surrounded by five volcanoes. Over 2,672 relief panels and 504 seated Buddha statues make it the greatest Buddhist monument on earth. Your guide will narrate the ancient Sailendra story as you ascend to the circular terraces with their iconic perforated stupas.
items.imageAlt: Borobudur temple upper terrace at dawn, Yogyakarta

items.title: Mount Merapi 4WD Jeep Lava Tour
items.body: Board an open 4WD jeep and charge up the southern slopes of active Mount Merapi. Visit the Kaliadem bunker partially buried by 2010 lava flows, navigate the otherworldly Batu Alien rock formation, and stop at the Merapi Museum to understand the volcano's fearsome history. One of the most thrilling experiences in all of Java.
items.imageAlt: 4WD jeep on Mount Merapi lava fields

items.title: Mangkunegaran Palace & Solo Heritage
items.body: The royal city of Surakarta (Solo), about 2 hours east of Yogyakarta, is home to the magnificent Mangkunegaran Palace — a smaller but exquisitely preserved rival to Yogyakarta's Kraton. Explore its European-influenced pendopo, royal regalia and Javanese dance costumes. Solo's Triwindu antique market and colonial shophouses make for wonderful post-palace wandering.

items.title: Sangiran Early Man Site — UNESCO World Heritage
items.body: One of the world's most important palaeoanthropological sites, Sangiran near Solo has yielded fossils of Homo erectus ('Java Man') dating back 1.5 million years — roughly 60% of all known Homo erectus specimens come from here. The modern museum presents the discoveries compellingly and is a genuine wow-factor for curious travellers.
items.imageAlt: Sangiran Early Man Site museum, Solo, Java

items.title: Parangtritis Beach Sunset & Obelix Sea View
items.body: Start your journey with Yogyakarta's most atmospheric coastal experience. The Obelix Sea View perched on the Menoreh Hills offers sweeping Indian Ocean panoramas, while Parangtritis Beach — with its dark volcanic sand and powerful Indian Ocean surf — delivers one of the most dramatic sunsets in Java. Optional horse riding and traditional carriage rides add local colour.
items.imageAlt: Parangtritis Beach sunset, Yogyakarta southern coast

days.title: Arrive Yogyakarta — Obelix Sea View & Parangtritis Beach
days.description: Your private driver-guide meets you at Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) with a welcome board. After a delicious lunch at a local Javanese restaurant, set off south towards the Indian Ocean. Stop first at the Obelix Sea View café and lookout point on the Menoreh Hills ridge — a favourite among Jogja locals for its spectacular views over paddy fields and the southern coast. Continue to the vast Parangtritis Beach, where the dark volcanic sand and powerful Indian Ocean surf create a scene unlike any Malaysian beach. Optional horse riding and traditional dokar carriage rides are available for own account. Watch the sun slip below the horizon before returning to the hotel for a warm dinner and overnight rest.
suggestedActivities: Sunset panorama photos at Obelix Sea View
Horse riding or dokar carriage at Parangtritis Beach (own expense)
Try fresh grilled corn and coconut drinks at the beach stalls
Check into your Malioboro-area hotel and explore the night market

days.title: Solo Heritage Day — Mangkunegaran Palace, Triwindu Market & Sangiran Early Man Site
days.description: After breakfast, depart early for Surakarta (Solo), approximately 2 hours east of Yogyakarta — Java's 'other royal city' and a cultural treasure in its own right. Begin at the Mangkunegaran Palace (Pura Mangkunegaran), the court of the Mangkunegaran royal family. Its grand European-influenced pendopo (pavilion) houses magnificent Javanese gamelan instruments, royal pusaka (heirlooms) and elaborate wayang puppet collections. Explore the inner sanctum and learn how Solo's royal court differs from Yogyakarta's. Next, browse the Triwindu Antique Market, a fascinating labyrinth of wayang puppets, keris daggers, Dutch colonial ceramics and vintage Javanese artefacts. After lunch at a Solo restaurant, head to the UNESCO-listed Sangiran Early Man Site, where fossils of Homo erectus (Java Man) dating 1.5 million years have been unearthed — representing approximately 60% of the world's known Homo erectus remains. The modern Sangiran Museum presents this extraordinary story in an engaging, family-friendly way. Return to Yogyakarta for dinner and overnight.
suggestedActivities: Hire a palace guide for the full Mangkunegaran royal story
Browse for keris daggers and wayang puppets at Triwindu Market
Visit the Sangiran Discovery Site excavation area
Sample Solo's famous nasi liwet and serabi pancakes

days.title: Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour — Borobudur Temple — Kotagede — Malioboro Street
days.description: An action-packed day begins with a 4WD jeep adventure on the slopes of Mount Merapi, one of the world's most active volcanoes. The open jeep bounces up lava fields solidified after the catastrophic 2010 eruption, stopping at the partially buried Kaliadem Bunker — where two rescue workers perished — and the surreal Batu Alien rock formation. The Merapi Mini Museum provides context on the volcano's devastating history. After the jeep tour, visit Borobudur, the world's largest Buddhist temple and Indonesia's premier archaeological site. Ascend the nine platforms to reach the iconic upper terraces with their 72 perforated stupas and sweeping views of the volcanic Kedu Plain. Enjoy a satisfying lunch near the temple grounds before heading to Kotagede, Yogyakarta's former royal capital famed for its traditional Javanese silverwork — you can watch artisans at work and purchase unique pieces. End the day strolling the colourful Malioboro Street, the beating heart of Jogja's batik and craft trade, before returning for dinner.
suggestedActivities: Sunrise jeep tour option for dramatic Merapi lighting (own arrangement)
Climb Borobudur's upper terraces for panoramic volcano views
Commission a custom silver piece at a Kotagede workshop
Stock up on batik and leather puppets at Malioboro Street

days.title: Free Leisure Time — Departure
days.description: Enjoy a leisurely hotel breakfast and make the most of your final free hours in Yogyakarta. Many guests choose to revisit Malioboro Street for last-minute batik shopping, visit a batik workshop for a hands-on experience, or simply relax at a café along Jalan Prawirotaman — Jogja's most charming boutique street. At the appropriate time, your private driver will transfer you to Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) for your flight home.
suggestedActivities: Morning batik workshop at a local studio (own expense)
Final shopping at Malioboro Street and Beringharjo Market
Breakfast at a Malioboro rooftop café
Visit Prambanan Temple independently if time allows (own expense)

sections.heading: 4 Days Yogyakarta Best-Seller Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body: The 4 Days Yogyakarta Best-Seller is consistently our most-booked Indonesia tour package among Malaysian travellers — and for good reason. This carefully balanced itinerary covers Yogyakarta's 'must-see' list (Borobudur, Mount Merapi, Malioboro) while adding a full-day excursion to Solo (Surakarta) that most competing packages skip entirely. Solo's Mangkunegaran Palace offers a glimpse of Javanese royal culture distinct from Yogyakarta's Kraton, while the Sangiran Early Man Site gives families and curious travellers a fascinating prehistoric dimension. The package is available at highly competitive group rates — making it an outstanding choice for family groups of 5 or more travelling from Kuala Lumpur, Penang, or Johor Bahru. All arrangements from airport transfer to hotel check-out are handled by Dhesu's experienced Indonesia team, leaving you free to simply enjoy the journey.

sections.heading: Sangiran UNESCO World Heritage Site Near Solo, Java
sections.body: While Borobudur and Prambanan dominate the UNESCO conversation in Central Java, Sangiran is equally significant from a global heritage perspective. Discovered in 1891 by Eugène Dubois and systematically excavated from the 1930s onwards, the Sangiran site has produced fossil remains of Homo erectus — widely known as 'Java Man' — spanning 1.5 million to 300,000 years ago. These fossils represent approximately 60% of the world's known Homo erectus collection, making Sangiran arguably the single most important palaeoanthropological site on the planet. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1996, the area today houses the modern Sangiran Early Man Site Museum with well-curated galleries explaining human evolution, the geological stratigraphy of the Bengawan Solo River valley, and the daily life of prehistoric Javanese communities. For families and travellers with an interest in natural history or archaeology, Sangiran is a genuine highlight of the Solo day trip and one of the most memorable parts of this 4-day package.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Indonesia from Malaysia?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enjoy visa-free entry to Indonesia for up to 30 days. Simply present your valid passport at immigration on arrival — no pre-registration required.

items.question: How far is Solo (Surakarta) from Yogyakarta?
items.answer: Solo is approximately 65 km east of Yogyakarta, about a 1.5 to 2-hour drive depending on traffic. Our private vehicle makes the journey comfortable, and the drive through Central Java's rice paddy landscapes is enjoyable in itself.

items.question: Is this package suitable for families with young children?
items.answer: Absolutely. The itinerary is well-paced with a mix of active (jeep tour, beach) and cultural (palace, museum) experiences. Borobudur and Sangiran are particularly popular with curious children. Our driver-guides are experienced with family groups and can adjust the pace accordingly.

items.question: What is the group discount structure?
items.answer: Groups of 5 or more adults receive RM100/person off the standard rate (RM1,188 at 3-star). Groups of 8 or more adults receive RM200/person off (RM1,088 at 3-star). 4-star prices have equivalent discounts. Contact us for corporate or large-group rates.

items.question: Can I upgrade my hotel to 4-star?
items.answer: Yes. The 4-star supplement is RM200 per person above the 3-star rate. 4-star properties include Swiss-Belbutique Yogyakarta and Jambuluwuk Malioboro — both centrally located near Malioboro Street and offering excellent facilities.

items.question: Are flights included?
items.answer: Flights are not included in the package price. The rate covers land arrangements only. We recommend AirAsia's direct KLIA2–YIA route. We can assist with flight recommendations on request.

items.question: What is the best time of year to visit Yogyakarta?
items.answer: Yogyakarta is a year-round destination. The dry season (May to October) offers the clearest skies and is ideal for Borobudur sunrise visits. The wet season (November to April) brings lush green landscapes and fewer crowds. The Merapi jeep tour runs year-round, though early morning trips offer the best visibility.
